sutta: the buddha said monks pursuing 5 things a lay man is the outcast of layman the dirt of laymen the off-scouring or rubbish of laymen what fight he is without faith without morals is a fortune teller by curious ceremonies he believes in luck not deeds he seeks outside the sangha for a gift-worthy person and there first offers service monks pursuing these 5 things a layman is the outcast of laymen the dirt of scouring of laymen monks pursuing 5 things a layman is a jewel of laymen the lily of laymen the lotus of laymen mud 5 he is with faith he has mortals he is not a fortune teller by curious ceremonies he believes in deeds not in luck he seeks the sangha first to offer service for saying that these 5 things a layman is the jewel of laymen the lily of laymen the lotus of laymen that's the end of the suta
commentary: this sutra although it mentions layman is is it is also applicable to lay women uh perhaps a translation should have been a lay person so a person a good lay person is 1 with faith in the triple gems secondly he has precepts he has sila he has precepts third 1 he is not a fortune teller nor does he go to a fortune teller to see his fortune and the fourth he believes in these in karma not in lung and the fifth 1 he offers service to the sangha of manga [Music]
